key_insight: The paper uses controlled synaptic decay in a feed-forward 3D reconstruction network as an artistic proxy for how memory loss degrades predictive priors and distorts perceived reality.
engineering_relevance: For computational science and materials/energy visualization, it suggests a new way to study robustness and failure modes by intentionally degrading model parameters to probe how representations collapse.
method: Induce controlled synaptic decay in a feed-forward 3D reconstruction model.
Use the model as a cognitive proxy for a silicon-based memory system.
Present the result as an interactive installation to visualize subjective forgetting.
evidence_signal: The abstract describes an interactive demo and conceptual framework, but it does not report quantitative datasets, benchmark metrics, or formal evaluation results.
watch_item: Because the work is primarily artistic and phenomenological, its claims may not generalize as a validated method for engineering analysis or neuroscience inference.
paper_title: Echoes of the Prior: A Computational Phenomenology of Forgetting
